title: Aegis AWS Landing Zone Configuration
description: Deployment-specific configuration contract. See ADR-004 for rationale.
$comment: The 'eks' section is optional — only environments that deploy an EKS platform layer need it. Not every account has a cluster.

name.description: Organization name prefix used in resource naming
Registered domain for AWS account root emails

email_pattern.description: Email pattern with {account} placeholder, e.g. aws-aegis-{account}@example.com

role.description: Region role: primary or disaster recovery

zones.description: Availability Zone names within the region

start_url.description: IAM Identity Center AWS access portal URL

region.description: Region where Identity Center is deployed

infra_repo_id.description: GitHub numeric repository ID for the infra repo (run: gh api repos/OWNER/REPO --jq .id). When set, OIDC trust policies pin to this immutable ID in addition to the textual repo name. Defense-in-depth against repo rename/transfer edge cases.

app_repo_id.description: GitHub numeric repository ID for the app repo (sister repo, e.g. aegis-core). Run: gh api repos/OWNER/REPO --jq .id. Same defense-in-depth role as infra_repo_id.

alert_emails.description: Email addresses for budget alert notifications

top_cidr.description: Top-level CIDR block for AWS IPAM

pools.description: Regional IPAM pool declarations keyed by region name

home_region.description: Control Tower home region (permanent, cannot change)

governed_regions.description: Regions governed by Control Tower guardrails

kms_key_alias.description: KMS key alias for CloudTrail and Config encryption

grafana_cloud.description: Grafana Cloud free tier observability backend (ADR-022). Optional — present this block to enable the platform observability stack (ESO, prometheus-operator-crds, kube-state-metrics, Grafana Alloy). Omit to deploy the EKS platform without observability. Runbook 006 documents the one-time signup producing these values.

org_slug.description: Grafana Cloud stack slug (matches URL path at <slug>.grafana.net)

mimir_url.description: Mimir Prometheus remote_write endpoint from stack details page. NOT derivable from org_slug — hostname varies by stack provisioning region.

mimir_user_id.description: Numeric user ID for Mimir HTTP basic auth, from stack details page

cognito.description: Cognito User Pool — cloud-mode auth for aegis (ADR-026). Optional — present this block to enable the staging/auth peer layer. Omit to deploy the landing zone without an auth layer. Runbook 008 documents first-operator setup including admin user creation and Hosted UI login verification. See ADR-026 §Decision for the custom:tenant_id attribute design.

callback_urls.description: App client allowed callback URL list — SPA OAuth redirect targets. aegis-core-confirmed default: ['https://aegis-app.staging.binhsu.org/auth/callback']. Cognito accepts in-place updates to this list without recreate.

logout_urls.description: App client allowed logout redirect URL list. aegis-core-confirmed default: ['https://aegis-app.staging.binhsu.org/']. Cognito accepts in-place updates without recreate.

domain_prefix.description: Cognito-provided hosted UI domain prefix — full URL is <prefix>.auth.<region>.amazoncognito.com. Globally unique within the region. Default: 'aegis-staging'. Changing this forces a ~30s hosted-UI 5xx window during resource recreate.

minimum_length.description: Minimum password length. Lab default 12 (NIST SP 800-63B aligned).

password_policy.description: Cognito password policy. Omit any subfield to use the lab default. Omit the whole block to inherit the full lab-default policy (min 12, all four character classes required).

mfa_configuration.description: MFA posture. OFF (lab default) = no MFA. OPTIONAL = users may enroll a factor and get prompted when present. ON = factor required for every user; login hard-fails if not configured. ON is not recommended for MVP — use OPTIONAL if you want to demo MFA without breaking lab-operator login.

qdrant_cloud.description: Qdrant Cloud vector-DB scaffold (ADR-025, ldz #141). Optional — present with `enabled: true` to provision 2 operator-managed SSM PS placeholders (/aegis/staging/qdrant-cloud/{cluster-url,api-key}) plus an ExternalSecret that reconciles them into K8s Secret `qdrant-credentials` in ns `aegis`. Values are filled operator-side via `aws ssm put-parameter` per Runbook 007; Terraform never sees the real credentials. Omit the block to skip Qdrant entirely.

enabled.description: Set true to provision the Qdrant SSM PS + ExternalSecret scaffold. False (or omit the block) skips all Qdrant resources.

eks.description: EKS platform configuration keyed by workload environment name (e.g. staging, prod). Optional — only environments that deploy a platform layer need an entry.

public_access_cidrs.description: CIDR blocks allowed to reach the EKS public API endpoint. See docs/runbooks/002-eks-access.md for the IP-drift operational contract.

regions.description: EKS compute footprint per env — must be a subset of top-level regions[]. Length 1 = single-region; length 2 = multi-region with primary + one slave. The maxItems=2 ceiling matches the slot-pattern K=2 declared in docs/decisions/018-multi-region-eks-design.md §3 'Scaling boundary'. To go beyond 2: amend ADR-018 §3 (raise K), add a `slave_2` provider alias + module invocation in staging/{network,platform}/ (mirroring slave_1), then bump both this maxItems and the terraform_data.assert_k2_max precondition in the two config.tf files. Cross-field invariants (exactly-one-primary, subset, uniqueness) validated by scripts/validate-config.py and Terraform check blocks.

region.description: AWS region name; must also appear in top-level regions[]

role.description: Cluster role: exactly one primary per env is required

mode.description: DR mode: active for primary; pilot_light or warm_standby for slaves (see ADR-018)

node_instance_types.description: Optional — Karpenter NodePool instance type list

id.description: AWS account ID (12 digits) or empty string if not yet provisioned

email.description: Root email address for the AWS account

vpcs.description: VPC declarations keyed by region name (IPAM-driven, see ADR-004 Mode B)

netmask_length.description: CIDR netmask length for IPAM allocation

ipam_pool.description: Reference to an IPAM pool name in ipam.pools
